was the first season of the [Namibia Premiership](/wiki/Namibia_Premiership "Namibia Premiership"), the top-tier football league in [Namibia](/wiki/Namibia "Namibia") which replaced the [Namibia Premier League](/wiki/Namibia_Premier_League "Namibia Premier League"), and the first full season of top-tier football in the country following the COVID-19 pandemic.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Football league season

[Namibia Premiership](/wiki/Namibia_Premiership "Namibia Premiership")

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Season | 2022–23 |
| Dates | 6 November 2022 – 13 May 2023 |
| Champions | [African Stars](/wiki/African_Stars "African Stars") |
| Relegated | [Eleven Arrows](/wiki/Eleven_Arrows_F.C. "Eleven Arrows F.C."), [Citizens](/wiki/Citizens_F.C. "Citizens F.C."),  [Black Africa](/wiki/Black_Africa_F.C. "Black Africa F.C.") |
| [Champions League](/wiki/2023%E2%80%9324_CAF_Champions_League "2023–24 CAF Champions League") | [African Stars](/wiki/African_Stars "African Stars") |
| Matches | 240[[1]](#cite_note-fb-1) |
| Goals | 608 (2.53 per match)[[1]](#cite_note-fb-1) |
| Top goalscorer | [Namibia](/wiki/Namibia "Namibia") [Willy Stephanus](/wiki/Willy_Stephanus "Willy Stephanus")  (20 goals) |
| ← [2018–19](/wiki/2018%E2%80%9319_Namibia_Premier_League "2018–19 Namibia Premier League") *(Premier League)*  [2023–24](/wiki/2023%E2%80%9324_Namibia_Premiership "2023–24 Namibia Premiership") → | |

The **2022–23 Namibia Premier Football League** was the first season of the [Namibia Premiership](/wiki/Namibia_Premiership "Namibia Premiership"), the top-tier football league in [Namibia](/wiki/Namibia "Namibia") which replaced the [Namibia Premier League](/wiki/Namibia_Premier_League "Namibia Premier League"), and the first full season of top-tier football in the country following the COVID-19 pandemic.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

The Namibia Premier League was dissolved after the 2018/19 season and there was no top flight football in the country until Debmarine Namibia sponsored the league for N$13 million. The league started in November 2022 with African Stars playing Unam FC in the first match.[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4)

[African Stars](/wiki/African_Stars_F.C. "African Stars F.C.") won the championship with several rounds still to play and qualified for the CAF Champions League.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

[![2022–23 Namibia Premier Football League is located in Namibia](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/fe/Namibia_location_map.svg/500px-Namibia_location_map.svg.png)](/wiki/File:Namibia_location_map.svg "2022–23 Namibia Premier Football League is located in Namibia")

## Controversy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2022%E2%80%9323_Namibia_Premier_Football_League&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Controversy")]

On 8th May 2023 Sylvester ‘Lolo’ Goraseb executive member of Black Africa replied to a Facebook post by the Thru Pass Football magazine that “there will be no promotion or relegation” while sitting 3rd from the bottom of the league.[[6]](#cite_note-6)
